01 · zopnight · engines

ZopNight
Cloud governance + FinOps.

Find waste. Schedule non-prod off. Right-size prod. Harden every launch. Agentless 2-min setup, 20–60% off the bill.

01 · Recommendations by Lens

Recommendations by Lens

AWS, GCP, and Azure advisors give everyone the same advice. Lens reads your accounts, your tags, your real bill and surfaces the dollars you can stop spending.

02 · Sequenced Scheduling

Sequenced Scheduling

Your dev environment runs 168 hours a week. Your team uses 50. Sequenced Scheduling schedules the other 118 in the right order, in the right timezone, automatically and reclaims the spend.

03 · Autoscaling by Tide

Autoscaling by Tide

Traffic at 3 PM Tuesday isn't traffic at 3 PM Sunday. Tide sizes your fleet for the moment, not the peak automatically.

04 · Event Readiness Engine New

Event Readiness Engine

Black Friday. Product launch. The marketing email that goes to two million users at noon. The Engine pre-scales the fleet, rolls back when the window closes. Be ready without sitting in idle headroom for weeks.

05 · Auto-Remediation

Auto-Remediation

From flagged to fixed. One click stops idle resources, scales workloads to zero, pauses what shouldn’t run. Every action audited, every change reversible.

More in ZopNight
01 · Architecture

Canvas + Atlas view

Map every cloud. Trace every dependency. Automatically across AWS, GCP, and Azure.

02 · Reports

Dashboards & reports

Boardroom-ready cost reports. Four dashboard presets. A Sankey that drills four levels deep into your bill.

03 · Tagging

Auto-tagging

Environment and stop-eligibility, predicted on every untagged resource. No tickets. No IaC migration.

04 · Showback

Showback & cost attribution

Every dollar attributed, by the teams that own it, the tags that classify it.

05 · Resources

All-resources view

Search, filter, and bulk-act on every resource, across every cloud account, three levels deep.

02 · zopnight · getting started

From read-only to remediated.
In under 10 minutes.

Four engines. One console. Click any step to see it play out in the product.

Right-size production
  1. Connect
    Read-only IAM across AWS, GCP, Azure. No agents, no sidecars.
  2. Scan
    450+ rules across eight categories: idle, right-sizing, schedule, orphan, compliance, discount, security, reliability.
  3. Recommend
    Every finding shows the dollar, the confidence, the why. Reconciled against your actual bill — Cost Explorer, Cost Management, BigQuery.
  4. Apply
    Remediate certified rules in one click. Dismiss or Mark Resolved for the rest. Every action stamped with actor and timestamp.
Reclaim non-prod hours
  1. Connect
    Read-only IAM across AWS, GCP, Azure. No agents, no sidecars.
  2. Discover
    30+ schedulable resource types, mapped on connect — VMs, Kubernetes clusters and workloads, managed databases, ECS, ASG, Databricks, EMR Serverless, App Runner, SageMaker, and more.
  3. Schedule
    Pick a template — Business Hours, Nights Only, Weekends Off, Dev Hours — or paint your own heatmap. Every schedule is IANA-timezone, DST-aware, and dependency-aware.
  4. Create
    Click Create. The schedule goes live. Watch the weekly savings — 50–65% off non-prod compute on eligible workloads.
Pre-flight the launch
  1. Event
    Name your event. Black Friday, product launch, billing run, the marketing email that goes to two million users at noon. One field, one description.
  2. Timing
    Set start, end, pre-scale, cooldown. Timeline preview shows the total scaled-up window before you commit.
  3. Targets
    Pick what to scale — existing autoscaler policy, resource group, or discovered resource. Set expected traffic multiplier. Flag databases to monitor, never modify.
  4. Review
    Full plan in one screen. Blockers and warnings surface upfront. Schedule when every check turns green.
Size for the moment
  1. Pick
    Pick the cloud account and the resource to scale — ASG, scale set, MIG, AKS pool, ECS service.
  2. Recommend
    14 days of CPU metrics — avg, P95, max — calibrate min, max, cooldown, and CPU target. Burst headroom built in. Use the recommended settings or customize.
  3. Mode
    Pick Autopilot or Manual. Autopilot applies scaling automatically on the triggers you define. Manual recommends; you apply on demand.
  4. Create
    Click Create. The policy goes live.
  1. 01
    Connect
    Read-only IAM across AWS, Azure, GCP. No agents, no sidecars.
  2. 02
    Recommendation
    Every finding ranked by dollar impact, with the resources behind it.
  3. 03
    Evidence
    Validated against your actual bill. The trace makes the saving real.
  4. 04
    Remediations
    ZopDay applies the fix safely, logs every action, ready to revert.

03 · zopday · platform

ZopDay.
Internal Developer Platform.

Spin up clusters. Push code to deploy. Operate every cluster from one workspace. From cloud credential to first deploy, same day.

01· Environments

One-click environments

K8s clusters across AWS, GCP, and Azure — EKS, GKE, AKS, one wizard. Or import the cluster you already run.

AWSEKS
GCPGKE
AzureAKS
02· Deployments

Push to deploy

Push code or a Docker image. ZopDay auto-builds it — no Dockerfile needed for Go, Node, Python, Java, Rust, PHP, Ruby. Helm rolls it out. One-click rollback.

03· YAML Templates

Templates, not YAML.

Set port, replicas, probes, CPU, memory, env vars in one place. Pick Ingress or LoadBalancer from a tab. ZopDay writes the Helm underneath.

Provision. Deploy. Operate. — From one workspace, on your cloud.

04 · zopday · getting started

Connect to ship.
From cloud account to live, in minutes.

Three flows. One CloudOps platform. Click any tab to watch it play out.

  1. Pick a cloud
    AWS EKS, GCP GKE, or Azure AKS. Or import a cluster you already run.
  2. Configure
    Cluster name, region, K8s version. New VPC or bring your own, same-region cross-VPC datastores auto-peer.
  3. Provision
    Pre-flight checks before each expensive call. When something fails, the actual cause surfaces, not “operation failed.”
  4. Reachable from minute zero
    Reachability probed every 60 seconds. If the cluster goes inactive, Sync and Install gate cleanly, no cryptic SDK errors.
  1. Connect a repo
    GitHub auto-deploy via HMAC webhook, or push a Docker image. Auto-detects Go, Node, Python, Java, Rust, PHP, Ruby.
  2. Configure the service
    Port, replicas, probe, CPU/memory, env vars (masked). Ingress (cert-manager TLS) or LoadBalancer, picked from a tab.
  3. Push and build
    Git push fires the pipeline. Railpack builds where there’s no Dockerfile; in-repo Dockerfile used as-is. Commit SHA on every revision.
  4. Helm rollout, with diagnostics
    Diagnostics in the UI when something fails. One-click rollback ships the previous revision’s image, build skipped. DORA-lite per environment.
  1. Pick the slot
    Application → Environment → Service. The Environment carries cluster, namespace, registry; services inherit.
  2. Configure the service
    Port, replicas, probe, CPU/memory, env vars (masked, per-row reveal). Ingress (cert-manager TLS) or LoadBalancer.
  3. Render
    ZopDay re-renders the Helm chart and triggers a new revision. No chart rewrite, no separate manifest.
  4. Clone forward
    Clone an Environment (staging → production); every service copies forward. Cascade teardown on delete, no orphaned releases.
Coming soon
  1. 01
    Connect
    AWS, GCP, Azure account and Kubernetes cluster connected in under a minute.
  2. 02
    Define
    Link your Git repo, Docker image, or Helm chart. No YAML to write.
  3. 03
    Deploy
    One click ships the build, push, and rollout across regions.
  4. 04
    Observe
    Logs, metrics, and traces for every service from a single dashboard.

05 · zopday · integrations

Integrations across your stack.

Every cloud, every tool. One credential.

13 · security

Built for teams that can't afford to get security wrong.

Read-only by default. Zero data storage. Every action logged, auditable, reversible.

01

Read-only by default

We observe and recommend. You approve and apply.

02

Zero data storage

Your cloud metadata stays in your account. We don't replicate it.

03

Audit trail

Every remediation timestamped, attributed, exportable.

04

Role-based access

Control who can view, recommend, and apply. Down to the resource.

05

Encryption

AES-256 at rest. TLS 1.3 in transit. Always.

This is the exact policy we ask for.

Read-only actions. No *:Write. No *:Delete. Remediation runs in a separate, explicitly-scoped role that you opt into per action.

Review it. Deploy it via Terraform. Rotate the role anytime. We've designed for your security team, not around them.

aws · iam azure · rbac gcp · iam
zop-read-only.json
# ZopNight discovery role · read-only across compute, storage, billing Version: "2012-10-17" Statement: - Effect: "Allow" Action: - "ec2:Describe*" - "rds:Describe*" - "s3:ListAllMyBuckets" - "s3:GetBucketTagging" - "cloudwatch:GetMetricData" - "ce:GetCostAndUsage" - "tag:GetResources" Resource: "*" Condition: StringEquals: aws:PrincipalOrgID: "o-ZopDev" # Apply-time actions live in a separate zop-remediate role · opt-in only

Certified.
Compliant.
Cloud-native.

Procure ZopNight through your existing cloud agreements. Use committed spend. Satisfy procurement in one click. Not one quarter.

SOC 2 Type II ISO 27001 GDPR Compliant HIPAA Ready
aws marketplace → azure marketplace → gcp marketplace →
section · the atlas

The map your cloud was supposed to come with.

Connect a cloud account. The atlas surfaces every resource, every relationship, every cost, automatically. No agents. No sidecars. Read-only by default.

What the atlas shows
Every resource380+ types across AWS, GCP, Azure, VMs, databases, K8s, serverless, storage, networking, ML, messaging
Every relationshipSubnet swimlanes, security-group hubs, three-level nesting (cluster → nodepool → VM), VPC peering, attached storage, queue triggers
Every costHourly rack rate + actual billing data from Cost Explorer, Cost Management, BigQuery, Azure amortized for reservations
Every postureCompliance, encryption, public access, IAM drift, overlaid on the same map
Two viewsGlobe for executive overview by region · Canvas for layered architectural deep-dive

Vantage shows costs. Datadog shows topology. Spot shows compute. ZopDev shows all of it, on one map.

No re-platform when you grow. No surprise bill when you scale. No drift when it’s running.

Underneath all three: ZopDev, the unified atlas, the shared identity model, the immutable audit trail, the MCP that talks to all of them.

section · evidence over opinion

Every recommendation ships with the receipts.

Most FinOps tools give you a list of suggestions and a percentage. ZopDev gives you the metric we observed, the threshold we measured against, the current cost, the optimized cost, and the exact remediation steps, manual by default. Auto-remediation only runs with explicit, scoped permission.

{
  "currentCost":   140.16,
  "optimizedCost":  70.08,
  "savings":        70.08,
  "evidence": {
    "metric":      "CPU",
    "value":        25.3,
    "threshold":    40.0,
    "lookbackDays": 7
  },
  "action":     "Downsize D4s_v3 → D2s_v3",
  "remediation": "1. Confirm workload off-peak window…"
}

Three remediation modes, your call:

ModeWhat it doesWho picks
MonitorSurfaces the finding, does nothing elseDefault. Always on.
RecommendShows manual remediation steps. You apply.Most enterprise customers.
AutopilotAuto-fixes where the action is safe and pre-approvedOpt-in per resource type, scoped per cloud account, fully logged.

No black-box ML. No “trust us.” Auditable evidence on every line item.

section · cdcr

CDCR, the loop that
keeps it fixed.

CI/CD made code continuous. CDCR makes your cloud continuous. Detect, remediate, audit, so cost and posture don’t drift back.

01
DETECT
Detect, continuously.

Drift on K8s clusters and schedules. Cost anomaly detection across five dimensions, org, account, group, resource, team. Expired overrides. Newly untagged resources. Compliance gaps that quietly reopened.

02
CLASSIFY
Classify, by impact.

450+ audit rules across AWS, GCP, and Azure. Every finding scored by severity and dollar impact, so production drift ranks above an idle dev box. The queue reflects actual stakes, not rule count.

03
REMEDIATE
Remediate, continuously.

Auto-fix where it’s safe, tag application, schedule enforcement, idle stops, scale-to-zero, pause. Guided remediation for the rest, with confidence and complexity scores up front. Production writes admin-gated and logged. Customer databases excluded from mutation.

04
VERIFY
Verify, every action.

Every action lands in the audit trail, actor, timestamp, dollar delta. Quarterly review reads measurable outcomes, never forecasts.

continuous
ACTIVE
UNIQUE TO CDCR

Most FinOps platforms detect and ticket. CDCR detects and acts.

section · event readiness

Score the cloud before the spike.

Define a launch, a load test, a Black Friday window. ZopDev runs a capacity calculator against your current scaling posture, recent activity, and the resources in the event’s scope. Per-scope verdicts tell you what’s ready, what to scale, what to provision, before the event, not during the page.

What gets scoredWhat you see
Compute (ASG, MIG, AKS pools)Ready / scale-up needed / capacity gap with delta
Databases (RDS, Aurora, Cloud SQL, Azure SQL, ElastiCache, Memorystore)DB-impact verdict + suggested headroom
Queues, caches, downstream servicesPer-target readiness check
Recent autoscaler signal14-day activity baseline

No competitor in the FinOps or observability stack offers this. Spike-day surprises become spike-day boredom.

section · ai-native by design

Your whole platform, in your editor.

ZopDev ships an MCP server with 43 tools spanning resources, schedules, costs, recommendations, deployments, topology, budgets, audit logs.

Connect Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex, or Claude Code in one PAT. Read-only by default; write actions scoped per role and append-only logged.

  • “What’s costing the most this week across our three clouds?”
  • “Score readiness for the May 15 launch.”
  • “Show me the topology for the payments service.”
  • “Roll back the staging deploy.”

section · built for regulated india

Engineered for the buyers who can't afford to get it wrong.

  • ISO 27001:2022 issued March 2025 · SOC 2 Type II annual third-party audit
  • IRDAI ICS 2023 + 2026 amendments controls mapped · DPDP Act 2023 DPA available · Outsourcing Reg 2017 contractual rights
  • GCP India (Mumbai) primary · MeitY-empaneled across AWS, Azure, GCP, OCI · sovereign Indian providers (CtrlS, Sify, NIC) buildable on request
  • Read-only by default. Write access scoped, opt-in, tag-conditioned. Production untouched until authorized.
  • AES-256-GCM credential encryption · per-account isolation · dedicated vault service
  • Full audit trail, every API call: actor, action, timestamp, body. Forwardable to your SIEM.
  • SaaS · VPC · on-prem, your choice

We are not in the data path of any policyholder, claim, or PII workload, by architecture.

faq

Questions we get a lot.

If yours isn't here, ask us directly.

Does ZopNight require agent installation or code changes? +

No agents, no sidecars, no Terraform changes. ZopNight connects through read-only cloud APIs. AWS, GCP, and Azure access is granted via IAM roles you provision once. Discovery starts in minutes, not days.

How quickly will I see findings? +

Most accounts see their first findings within 2–3 minutes of connecting. The initial scan covers all resources across regions. After that, ZopNight runs continuously. New waste is surfaced as it appears, not on a daily batch.

What does "one-click remediation" actually do? +

When you click Fix →, ZopNight calls the cloud provider API directly. Stopping an idle instance, deleting an orphaned volume, rightsizing a reservation. Every action is logged, reversible where the cloud allows it, and you can set approval workflows so nothing runs without a human sign-off.

Which clouds and services are supported? +

AWS, GCP, and Azure. Covering compute, storage, databases, networking, and Kubernetes across all regions. We add new service coverage every sprint. If you use something niche, tell us and it's usually on the roadmap within a quarter.

Is my cloud data safe? What does ZopNight read? +

ZopNight operates with least-privilege read access plus the specific write permissions for remediation actions you explicitly enable. We never read application data, secrets, or user content. Only resource metadata and billing data. All data is encrypted in transit and at rest. SOC 2 Type II report available on request.

Does it work alongside Terraform or other IaC tools? +

Yes. ZopNight detects IaC-managed resources and marks them separately so you know which fixes should go through your Terraform pipeline versus which can be actioned directly. For IaC resources, we can generate a diff-ready Terraform snippet rather than acting directly.

How is ZopNight priced? +

Pricing is based on the number of cloud resources managed. Not on savings found or actions taken. Most teams pay back the subscription cost in the first week. See full plans on the pricing page, or start free with the playground. No card required.

end · ready when you are

Bring your cloud.
